2605.06416.title: MiA-Signature: Approximating Global Activation for Long-Context Understanding
2605.06416.paper_summary: A growing body of work in cognitive science suggests that reportable conscious access is associated with \emph{global ignition} over distributed memory systems, while such activation is only partially accessible as individuals cannot directly access or enumerate all activated contents. This tension suggests a plausible mechanism that cognition may rely on a compact representation that approximates the global influence of activation on downstream processing. Inspired by this idea, we introduce the concept of \textbf{Mindscape Activation Signature (MiA-Signature)}, a compressed representation of the global activation pattern induced by a query. In LLM systems, this is instantiated via submodular-based selection of high-level concepts that cover the activated context space, optionally refined through lightweight iterative updates using working memory. The resulting MiA-Signature serves as a conditioning signal that approximates the effect of the full activation state while remaining computationally tractable. Integrating MiA-Signatures into both RAG and agentic systems yields consistent performance gains across multiple long-context understanding tasks.
2605.06416.paper_summary_zh: 越來越多的認知科學研究表明，可報告的意識訪問與分佈式記憶系統中的 \emph{全球點燃} 相關，而這種激活只有部分可訪問，因為個體無法直接訪問或列舉所有激活的內容。這種緊張關係暗示了一種合理的機制，即認知可能依賴於一種緊湊的表示，該表示近似於激活對下游處理的全球影響。受到這一想法的啟發，我們引入了 \textbf{心靈景觀激活簽名 (MiA-Signature)} 的概念，這是一種由查詢引起的全球激活模式的壓縮表示。在 LLM 系統中，這是通過基於子模塊的高層次概念選擇來實現的，這些概念覆蓋了激活的上下文空間，並可選擇通過使用工作記憶的輕量級迭代更新進行細化。最終生成的 MiA-Signature 作為一種信號，近似於完整激活狀態的效果，同時仍然保持計算上的可行性。將 MiA-Signatures 整合到 RAG 和代理系統中，能在多個長上下文理解任務中實現一致的性能提升。
2605.06416.author: Yuqing Li et.al.
2605.06416.authors: Yuqing Li, Jiangnan Li, Mo Yu, Zheng Lin, Weiping Wang, Jie Zhou
